description: CodeStubAssembler::LoadBuiltin in src/codegen/code-stub-assembler.cc validates the builtin ID index with only a CSA_DCHECK (a debug-only assertion stripped in release builds) rather than a CSA_SBXCHECK (a sandbox-enforced check). This is inconsistent with the C++ counterpart SharedFunctionInfo::builtin_id() in src/objects/shared-function-info-inl.h, which uses SBXCHECK(Builtins::IsBuiltinId(id)) to enforce bounds in all builds. When GetSharedFunctionInfoCode processes an untrusted Smi value from a SharedFunctionInfo's untrusted data field, it passes that Smi directly to LoadBuiltin without a sandbox bounds check. An attacker with in-sandbox write capability can corrupt the untrusted data field of a SharedFunctionInfo with an out-of-bounds Smi builtin ID. When the function is subsequently called, LoadBuiltin performs an out-of-bounds read past the builtin table, returning an attacker-controlled value as a Code object pointer. Because this forged Code object resides in writable V8 heap memory, the attacker can craft a mismatched parameter count, causing the stack to become imbalanced, which leads to arbitrary memory write (AAW) and PC control — a full V8 sandbox escape.

fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://chromium-review.googlesource.com/c/v8/v8/+/7045784 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
